Title: Nitrogen-dependent binding of the transcription factor PBF1 contributes to the balance of protein and carbohydrate storage in maize endosperm

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major storage carbohydrate in endosperm; seed energy reserve
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PBF1 directly represses Bt1 promoter activity in transient assay; Bt1 is a PBF1-bound target under both SN and DN, but its transcript level is not significantly altered by nitrogen deficiency.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bt1 was identified as a direct PBF1 target by ChIP-seq. 2. EMSA supported PBF1 binding to the Bt1 promoter P-box region. 3. Dual-luciferase assay showed PBF1 represses the Bt1 promoter, while ZmbZIP22 activates it. 4. Bt1 expression itself was relatively stable across SN and DN, indicating common PBF1-bound targets are less nitrogen-responsive.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bt1 mediates ADP-glucose transport into endosperm plastids and is important for starch biosynthesis, but in this study it served mainly as a representative direct target of PBF1 rather than the primary causal gene.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmWCR1 was bound and modulated by PBF1 under both SN and DN, but its transcript level was not significantly altered by nitrogen deficiency.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmWCR1 was a common PBF1 direct target under both nitrogen conditions. 2. EMSA supported PBF1 binding to the ZmWCR1 promoter. 3. Its expression remained relatively stable under DN versus SN, suggesting some PBF1 targets are nitrogen-insensitive.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is an experimentally discussed direct PBF1 target affecting grain quality context, but not a central nutrient-metabolism catalyst or regulator in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed carbon storage and energy reserve
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: branched glucans / nascent amylopectin; malto-oligosaccharides-related glucans
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: trimmed glucans and malto-oligosaccharides that support starch granule formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Debranches nascent amylopectin/glucans to generate malto-oligosaccharides and support proper starch granule formation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for proper starch synthesis and granule formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in developing endosperm around 15 DAP; upregulated under DN; elevated in pbf1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: sufficient nitrogen (SN) versus deficient nitrogen (DN) during kernel development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Su1 is directly repressed by PBF1 under SN; loss of PBF1 binding under DN coincides with increased Su1 expression, contributing to enhanced starch accumulation under nitrogen deficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Su1 was identified as a PBF1 direct target by ChIP-seq. 2. EMSA and ChIP-qPCR confirmed PBF1 binding to the Su1 promoter P-box region. 3. RNA-seq and RT-qPCR showed Su1 is upregulated under DN and in pbf1 mutants. 4. Dual-luciferase assays showed PBF1 represses the Su1 promoter and suppresses ZmbZIP22/ZmbZIP46-mediated activation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; CRISPR knockout; transient dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper highlights Su1 as a representative carbon-metabolism target through which PBF1 redirects carbon flux away from zein under SN and releases repression under DN.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed carbon storage and energy reserve
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces α-1,6-branch points into glucan chains during amylopectin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in developing endosperm around 15 DAP; induced by DN; repressed by PBF1 under SN
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Sbe2b is directly repressed by PBF1 under SN; under N deficiency, reduced promoter binding is associated with elevated Sbe2b expression and increased starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sbe2b was identified as a direct PBF1 target. 2. EMSA supported PBF1 binding to the Sbe2b promoter. 3. Dual-luciferase assay showed PBF1 represses the Sbe2b promoter and antagonizes ZmbZIP22/ZmbZIP46 activation. 4. The gene is highlighted as part of the mechanism by which PBF1 balances starch versus protein deposition in endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-seq; EMSA; CRISPR knockout; transient dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly emphasized in abstract and discussion as a core starch-synthesis gene repressed by PBF1.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed carbon storage and energy reserve
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: malto-oligosaccharide extension / branched glucan synthesis support
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: extended glucans / malto-oligosaccharides depending on reaction direction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates with SBEs in extension of malto-oligosaccharides and synthesis of branched glucans in maize endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Pho1 is a direct PBF1 target whose promoter is repressed by PBF1 in transient assays; its expression is among starch-biosynthetic genes elevated under DN.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pho1 promoter contains a PBF1-binding site validated by EMSA. 2. Pho1 was identified among starch metabolism genes regulated by PBF1 and responsive to nitrogen. 3. PBF1 repressed Pho1 promoter activity in transient expression assays.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-seq; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses PHO1 as functioning together with SBEs in synthesis of branched glucans.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides carbon skeletons for amino acid and storage protein biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: carbon metabolism linked to amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the reversible conversion of pyruvate, ATP, and Pi to phosphoenolpyruvate, AMP, and PPi.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for amino acid synthesis and influences carbon partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established; discussed as not the master switch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: major endosperm PPDK isoform; more highly expressed than PPDK1 in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PPDK2 is a PBF1-bound target under SN only, but its expression changes little under nitrogen deficiency; it is involved in carbon/nitrogen metabolism but is not supported here as the master switch between starch and protein accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPDK2 promoter is bound by PBF1 under SN. 2. PPDK2 transcript is abundant in endosperm but only slightly reduced under DN. 3. The paper argues PPDKs are involved in C/N metabolism but do not solely determine starch-protein partitioning.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is repeatedly discussed as a direct or relevant PBF1 metabolic target, but the paper explicitly downplays it as the master switch.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides carbon skeletons for amino acid and storage protein biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: carbon metabolism linked to amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes reversible pyruvate to phosphoenolpyruvate conversion, linking carbon flux to amino acid synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: lower than PPDK2 in endosperm; may increase under DN
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PPDK1 is bound by PBF1 under both SN and DN but its expression is not a major determinant of N-responsive starch/protein balance in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPDK1 is a common PBF1 direct target under both N conditions. 2. Its transcript may rise under DN, but significance is uncertain. 3. Together with PPDK2, it is discussed as participating in C/N metabolism rather than functioning as the decisive switch.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed extensively as a candidate switch from prior models, but current evidence argues against a master-switch role.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: protein and starch deposition determine kernel nutritional quality, endosperm hardness/vitreousness, and seed reserve composition.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dof family; DNA-binding one zinc finger; P-box-binding factor
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: storage protein biosynthesis/accumulation and starch biosynthesis during endosperm development
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: nitrogen-dependent differential DNA binding; activation of storage protein genes; repression of starch genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: nitrogen availability / sufficient N versus deficient N
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: zein genes including 27-kD γ-zein and multiple 19-kD/22-kD α-zeins; Su1; Sbe2b; Sbe1; Pho1; Bt1; PPDK1; PPDK2; ZmWCR1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates multiple zein genes and some amino acid metabolism genes; represses starch metabolism genes such as Su1, Sbe2b, Bt1, and Pho1 under SN; binding to many zein promoters is lost under DN
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: cooperates with O2 on a subset of shared targets; functionally antagonizes/attenuates ZmbZIP22 and ZmbZIP46 activation on starch-gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: endosperm-specific, mainly starchy endosperm at 15 DAP; transcript and protein abundance remain similar under SN and DN
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrogen deficiency versus sufficient nitrogen during kernel development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PBF1 reduces zein and total protein accumulation, increases starch accumulation, produces smaller opaque kernels, and attenuates the endosperm response to changing nitrogen supply.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: PBF1 is the key nitrogen-responsive transcriptional regulator identified in this study that rebalances carbon and nitrogen storage in maize endosperm by changing its binding spectrum rather than its abundance.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBF1 is the primary regulator linking nitrogen availability to maize endosperm protein/starch balance. 2. CRISPR knockout of Pbf1 caused reduced zein and protein accumulation, increased starch, and opaque kernels. 3. PBF1 transcript and protein abundance were stable under SN and DN, but ChIP-seq showed its promoter-binding spectrum changed strongly with nitrogen supply. 4. Under SN, PBF1 activates zein genes and represses starch genes such as Su1 and Sbe2b; under DN, loss of binding to many targets coincides with reduced zein expression and increased starch gene expression. 5. PBF1 cooperates with O2 on some targets and can suppress ZmbZIP22/ZmbZIP46-mediated activation of starch genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; immunoblot; RNA in situ hybridization; RT-qPCR; transient dual-luciferase assay; metabolite/protein profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PBF1 is a strong candidate for engineering kernel nutritional quality and starch/protein balance. Potential strategies include modifying PBF1 DNA-binding specificity to maintain 27-kD γ-zein while reducing α-zein, or manipulating N management together with PBF1-regulated networks. Caution: complete loss of PBF1 reduces yield and kernel vitreousness/quality.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: pbf1 mutants show smaller opaque kernels, reduced kernel weight, reduced storage protein accumulation, and likely yield penalties; broad perturbation of starch/protein balance may affect endosperm texture.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes that PBF1 binding state, not abundance, is N-responsive. Possible mechanisms discussed include phosphorylation status and chromatin accessibility changes under N stress. Anti-PBF1 ChIP-seq datasets are under PRJNA837716; RNA-seq under PRJNA602742.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for effector expression in transient assays; native target promoters in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No direct epigenetic regulation of Pbf1 was demonstrated; discussion proposes chromatin accessibility/methylation changes under N deficiency may alter PBF1 binding distribution.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: storage proteins affecting kernel nutritional quality and endosperm traits
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: storage protein biosynthesis and endosperm metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; cooperative regulation with PBF1 on some targets
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: some zein genes; PPDK1/2; SSIII and other O2 targets from prior studies
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functional cooperation with PBF1 on a subset of common targets
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: O2 shares some promoter targets with PBF1, but the overlap is limited; the main regulatory spectra of O2 and PBF1 are largely independent in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important known endosperm regulator but not the main focus of the present study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Comparison with published O2 ChIP-seq datasets identified 215 common genes bound by both O2 and PBF1. 2. Shared direct targets include some zein genes and PPDK1/2. 3. The study concludes O2 and PBF1 jointly regulate a minority of targets, with largely independent broader target sets.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as an experimentally analyzed interacting regulator in the Results, though not the central focus. O2 is background-supported and compared directly to PBF1 using published ChIP-seq.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: activation of starch synthesis gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; activation attenuated by PBF1 coexpression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: reported interaction with PBF1 from prior studies; functional coexpression in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmbZIP22 activates starch synthesis gene promoters, but coexpression with PBF1 represses Su1, Sbe2b, and Pho1 promoter activity relative to ZmbZIP22 alone.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: auxiliary regulator of starch genes used to reveal how PBF1 can suppress positive starch regulators
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In transient assays, ZmbZIP22 activated Su1, Sbe2b, Bt1, and Pho1 promoters. 2. Coexpression of PBF1 reduced ZmbZIP22-driven activation of Su1, Sbe2b, and Pho1. 3. This supports a model in which PBF1 can repress starch-gene expression partly by counteracting bZIP activators.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary nutrient regulator discovery in this paper, but experimentally central to the mechanism of PBF1-mediated repression.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: activation of starch synthesis gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; activation attenuated by PBF1 coexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmbZIP46 activates Su1 and Sbe2b promoters, and PBF1 significantly represses this activation when coexpressed.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: supporting regulator showing PBF1 can suppress positive starch regulators
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmbZIP46 transactivated Su1 and Sbe2b promoters in transient assays. 2. Coexpression with PBF1 reduced that activation. 3. The result supports the idea that PBF1 can act as a suppressor of other activators on starch genes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented in supplemental mechanistic assays; secondary to PBF1 but still experimentally tested in the Results.
